title: Add ScheduledItem, LogEntry, Goal.kind, and Goal GitHub-link fields via additive Prisma migration
value: so that all subsequent epics have the DB tables and Goal columns they need to build on, with zero disruption to existing fitness data
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds Goal.kind String @default("fitness") with @@index([kind])
prisma/schema.prisma adds Goal.githubRepo String? and Goal.githubProjectNumber Int? as nullable columns

prisma/schema.prisma adds the ScheduledItem model exactly as specified in plan-blueprint.md §1b: id, goalId FK (onDelete Cascade), date DateTime, type String, title String, detail String?, payload Json?, status String @default("planned"), completedAt DateTime?, externalRef String?, createdAt, updatedAt — with @@index([goalId,date]), @@index([goalId,status]), @@unique([goalId,externalRef])

prisma/schema.prisma adds the LogEntry model exactly as specified in plan-blueprint.md §1c: id, goalId FK (onDelete Cascade), date DateTime, metric String, value Float?, text String?, payload Json?, source String?, createdAt — with @@index([goalId,metric,date]) and @@index([goalId,date])
Goal model gains a scheduledItems ScheduledItem[] and logEntries LogEntry[] relation field

Migration additive: npx prisma migrate diff --from-schema-datasource prisma/schema.prisma --to-schema-datasource prisma/schema.prisma (against pre-change snapshot) shows only CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN — no DROP, no ALTER TYPE
npx prisma migrate dev --name multi-domain-spine runs without error against the Neon DB
npx prisma generate succeeds and src/generated/prisma contains ScheduledItem and LogEntry types
npx tsc --noEmit passes (no new type errors introduced)
npm run build passes
Existing Goal rows have kind='fitness' by default — verified via prisma studio or a quick SELECT

title: Add required goalId param to computeReadiness and computeReadinessSeries and update all 5 call sites so fitness readiness stays byte-identical
value: so that log:* metrics (added in the next story) are scoped to the correct goal and cannot cross-contaminate a fitness goal's readiness score with another goal's LogEntry rows
acceptanceCriteria: computeReadiness signature changes to: computeReadiness(targets: GoalTarget[], asOf: Date = new Date(), goalId: string) — goalId is REQUIRED (not optional) to prevent silent fallback to no-filter
computeReadinessSeries signature changes to: computeReadinessSeries(goalCreatedAt: Date, targets: GoalTarget[], now: Date = new Date(), goalId: string) — goalId REQUIRED
computeReadiness passes goalId through to resolveMetricValue on every call inside its targets loop
computeReadinessSeries passes goalId through to computeReadiness on every recursive cursor call

resolveMetricValue signature changes to: resolveMetricValue(prisma, metric, asOf, goalId: string) — REQUIRED param; existing fitness metric branches (weightLb, baseline:*, hike:*, workout:count) accept but ignore goalId — their DB queries are unchanged
resolveMetricStart signature changes to: resolveMetricStart(prisma, metric, goalId: string) — REQUIRED; existing branches ignore it

stats/page.tsx: computeReadiness(targets) call at line 39 updated to computeReadiness(targets, undefined, g.id); computeReadinessSeries(g.createdAt, targets) at line 40 updated to computeReadinessSeries(g.createdAt, targets, undefined, g.id)
progress/page.tsx: same two call sites (lines 37-38) updated identically with g.id
goals/[id]/page.tsx: computeReadiness(targets) at line 80 updated to computeReadiness(targets, undefined, goal.id)
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors — TypeScript strict-mode errors on all missing goalId arguments are the compiler signal that no call site was missed
npm run build passes
Browser smoke: /stats and /progress pages load and display the existing fitness goal's readiness score identically to before this change — score value unchanged, breakdown identical
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem, LogEntry, Goal.kind, and Goal GitHub-link fields via additive Prisma migration

title: Implement log:* metric namespace in resolveMetricValue, resolveMetricStart, and progressFor, and register log:mrr and log:milestones_done in the METRICS registry
value: so that project-goal targets like log:mrr and log:milestones_done are scoreable by the readiness engine with correct historical fidelity, and fitness readiness is byte-identical
acceptanceCriteria: resolveMetricValue: new branch for metric.startsWith("log:") executes a prisma.logEntry.findFirst({ where: { goalId, metric: key, date: { lte: asOf }, value: { not: null } }, orderBy: { date: "desc" } }) and returns entry?.value ?? null — goalId is used in the WHERE clause
resolveMetricStart: new branch for metric.startsWith("log:") returns 0 immediately (build-from-zero start, same as hike:* and workout:count)
progressFor: build-from-zero guard updated to also match target.metric.startsWith("log:") — the three conditions are: hike:*, workout:count, log:* — existing fitness conditions unchanged

METRICS array in goal-targets.ts gains exactly two new entries: { id: "log:mrr", label: "Monthly recurring revenue", units: "$", direction: "increase", description: "Latest MRR snapshot from a LogEntry." } and { id: "log:milestones_done", label: "Milestones completed", units: "milestones", direction: "increase", description: "Count of completed milestones, logged via log_goal_metric." }

For a fitness goal (no log:* targets): computeReadiness output is byte-identical — verified by calling the function with the existing Mt. Elbert targets and confirming score and breakdown are unchanged

For a project goal with a log:mrr target: computeReadiness returns progress=0 when no LogEntry rows exist (start=0, current=null → null progress), and returns correct fractional progress when a LogEntry(metric="mrr",value=500) exists against a target of 1000
No new LogEntry table query is executed for any fitness metric branch (weightLb, baseline:*, hike:*, workout:count) — only the log:* branch queries LogEntry
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm run build passes
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem, LogEntry, Goal.kind, and Goal GitHub-link fields via additive Prisma migration
Add required goalId param to computeReadiness and computeReadinessSeries and update all 5 call sites so fitness readiness stays byte-identical

title: Expose Goal.kind on create_goal, list_goals, and get_today_plan MCP tools
value: so that Claude can route to the correct tool pack from the very first turn of a session, without needing a separate get_goal call
acceptanceCriteria: create_goal tool input schema gains a kind field: z.enum(["fitness", "project"]).default("fitness").describe("Goal domain; determines which tool pack Claude uses. fitness = workout/hike/baseline tools; project = schedule_item/log_goal_metric/GitHub tools.") — persisted to Goal.kind on create
list_goals tool response includes kind in each goal object alongside the existing id, objective, targetDate, status, active fields

get_today_plan tool response gains an activeGoal field: { id: string, kind: string, objective: string, githubRepo: string | null } — populated from prisma.goal.findFirst({ where: { active: true }, orderBy: { updatedAt: "desc" } }); null if no active goal
Existing fitness goal created without kind defaults to kind="fitness" — verified by calling create_goal without kind and confirming the stored row has kind="fitness"
curl smoke: tools/call get_today_plan returns activeGoal.kind="fitness" for the existing Mt. Elbert goal
curl smoke: tools/call list_goals returns kind for each goal in the array
curl smoke: tools/call create_goal with kind="project" stores kind="project" (verify via get_goal)
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm run build passes
MCP connector reload in claude.ai required after merge — noted in PR/commit message
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem, LogEntry, Goal.kind, and Goal GitHub-link fields via additive Prisma migration
